message:
🌱 Add n8n: Incorrect MIME Type when reading file using “Read/Write mission (#1522)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "n8n-22007-incorrect-mime-type-when-reading-file-using-read-write-files-from-disk",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "n8n: Incorrect MIME Type when reading file using “Read/Write Files from Disk” node",
+    "description": "Incorrect MIME Type when reading file using “Read/Write Files from Disk” node. Community-requested feature.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current n8n deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your n8n version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
helm list -n n8n 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working n8n installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review n8n configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant n8n configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get configmap -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=n8n
```
### Bug Description

Hello,,

After updating n8n to the beta version, the `Read/Write Files from Disk` node returns a different MIME type than in the previous version (1.120.3)."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Incorrect MIME Type when reading file using “Read/Write…",
+        "description": "I’ve been running into this issue recently as well.

I’m currently on **n8n 2.1.1**, and what’s confusing is that **some videos upload successfully while others fail with the same workflow**. At first I thought it might be a pure n8n version issue, but around the same time I also changed some
```yaml
{
  "nodes": [
    {
      "parameters": {
        "operation": "write",
        "fileName": "/n8n_data/output.mp4",
        "dataPropertyName": "data0",
        "options": {}
      },
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.readWriteFile",
      "typeVersion": 1,
      "position": [
        656,
        208
      ],
      "id": "1786827e-1a17-4273-8c14-c0f6480e06a2",
      "name": "Read/Write Files from Disk1"
    },
    {
      "parameters": {
        "options": {
          "allowFileUploads": true
        }
      },
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.chatTrigger",
      "typeVersion": 1.4,
     
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Upgrade n8n to include the fix",
+        "description": "If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade n8n:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade n8n n8n/n8n --namespace n8n
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n
helm list -n n8n
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n n8n -l app.kubernetes.io/name=n8n
kubectl get events -n n8n --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Incorrect MIME Type when reading file using “Read/Write…" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "I’ve been running into this issue recently as well.

I’m currently on **n8n 2.1.1**, and what’s confusing is that **some videos upload successfully while others fail with the same workflow**. At first I thought it might be a pure n8n version issue, but around the same time I also changed some **ffmpeg options used to generate the videos**, so that might be part of the trigger too.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "{
  "nodes": [
    {
      "parameters": {
        "operation": "write",
        "fileName": "/n8n_data/output.mp4",
        "dataPropertyName": "data0",
        "options": {}
      },
      "type": "n8n-nodes-base.readWriteFile",
      "typeVersion": 1,
      "position": [
        656,
        208
      ],
      "id": "1786827e-1a17-4273-8c14-c0f6480e06a2",
      "name": "Read/Write Files from Disk1"
    },
    {
      "parameters": {
        "options": {
          "allowFileUploads": true
        }
      },
      "type": "@n8n/n8n-nodes-langchain.chatTrigger",
      "typeVersion": 1.4,
      "position": [
        432,
        208
      ],
      "id": "c6548e10-5d69-4019-9ae4-542003e532f3",
      "name": "When chat message received",
      "webhookId": "b473a745-8d04-4d83-bb7d-79a07edf99b",
+        "{
  "nodes": [
    {
      "parameters": {
        "jsCode": "// Récupère le premier item avec binary data\nconst item = $input.first();\n\nif (!item.binary || !item.binary.data) {\n  throw new Error('Pas de données binaires trouvées');\n}\n\n// Récupère les données binaires ORIGINALES (Buffer)\nconst originalBinary = $input.first().binary.data;\n\n// Crée une NOUVELLE entrée binaire avec MIME type corrigé\nconst newBinaryData = {\n  data: originalBinary.data,  // Buffer original (CRUCIAL)\n  mimeType: 'video/mp4',      // ✅ CORRECTION ICI\n  fileName: originalBinary.fileName || 'Saint_Andre.mp4',\n  fileExtension: 'mp4',\n  fileSize: originalBinary.fileSize\n};\n\nreturn [{\n  json: {\n    originalMime: originalBinary.mimeType,\n    newMime: 'video/mp4',\n    fileSize: originalBinary.file"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
